title: Perlin Noise Terrain Sandbox
description: A procedural terrain generator using native 2D Perlin noise with fBm octave synthesis, radial island masking, real-time parameter modulation (sea level, peak scale, noise scale, octaves, persistence), theme-based color mapping, and PNG export.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: Native Perlin noise: self-contained implementation with permutation table (seed-shuffled via LCG RNG), 8-direction gradient dot product, quintic fade function 6t⁵-15t⁴+10t³, bilinear interpolation
fBm octave synthesis: stacked noise layers with frequency doubling per octave, amplitude scaled by persistence, normalized by maxVal for [-1,1] → [0,1] mapping
Radial island masking: smoothstep (3m²-2m³) distance attenuation from center, generating self-contained coastal landmasses
Direct pixel buffer rendering: ctx.createImageData → Uint8ClampedArray → ctx.putImageData with color mapped from 6-band cyberpunk spectrum (deep blue → cyan → amber → emerald → gray → white)
Real-time slider modulation: sea level (0.20-0.60), peak scale (0.5-2.0 power curve), noise scale (1-10 frequency), octaves (1-6), persistence (0.2-0.8) — all trigger regeneration with 50ms debounce
3 theme presets: Cyberpunk (neon), Mars (rust/terracotta), Alien (purple/cyan) — each defines 6-band color palette with smooth lerp transitions
Seed management: newSeed() generates random hash via Math.random()×2³¹, shuffles permutation table, regenerates terrain; seed hash displayed in telemetry
PNG export: canvas.toDataURL('image/png') triggers downloadable terrain_seed_{hash}.png via programmatic <a> click
Telemetry: land-to-water ratio, highest peak altitude (max height value), active seed hash — updated per generate()
